description: §19.2.2 renders-clause payload access — a `renders` clause references MULTIPLE payload fields of its variant. charge(75) fails PaymentError.InsufficientFunds(50, 75); the variant's renders <div class="eb-funds">Need ${requested} but only ${available} available</> substitutes BOTH bound fields, winning over the boundary fallback (§19.6.5 priority #1). OQ1: whole-tree dom OMITTED (private boundary container); anchored contract only.
rationale: §19.2.2: a `renders` clause body has access to the variant's payload fields. InsufficientFunds carries {available, requested}; the clause interpolates both -> 'Need 75 but only 50 available'. §19.6.5 priority #1: the variant's renders wins over the boundary fallback (eb-fb never appears). E-ERROR-* silent (boundary satisfies handling).
